title: What was Seele trying to achieve with Adam CE Second Impact
display_title: What was Seele trying to achieve with Adam CE Second Impact
wikitext:
[]: What was Seele trying to achieve with Adam CE/Second Impact?             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Sun Nov 10, 2013 3:23 pm Hello! I felt this might have been discussed before, so I did my homework and Ctrl+F the heck out of the thread list in the discussion forum looking for Seele, 2I and Second Impact. Now, a lot of people contributed to a lot of details when it comes to the mechanics of the actual event, some very interesting speculations and implications (like the donor being a 14 yo whose body received Adam's soul and became Kaworu, or the Angels being created and receiving a soul through the Chamber of Guf opening and so on). But I'm still not sure why Seele decided to go ahead with the contact experiment. What were they trying to achieve? Did they need to get Adam create the Angels so that they could fight them and prove to be the worthy candidates for godhood? Where they just trying to destroy Adam and were they hoping that its killing wouldn't make THAT much of a mess? We seem to assume that Seele knew what was going to happen because Gendo left just before 2I. What if he did that out of his own initiative/paranoia and not by an order of Keel? Did they just need a cover story to give Angels a bad name and justify the existence and use of Evas once the remaining Angels would start to attack (but before then, the meteorite was ok as a cover up)? I'm not really sure...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 10, 2013 3:48 pm They were essentially trying to follow a script exactly. Even if it were possible to do X without doing Y, the script says Y comes before X, and they're following it zealously.

[]: Re: What was Seele trying to achieve with Adam CE/Second Imp             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 10, 2013 3:53 pm Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: some very interesting speculations and implications (like the donor being a 14 yo whose body received Adam's soul and became Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 10, 2013 3:54 pm SEELE can't launch HIP until all the Angels are dealt with, So setting up 2I was more or less the announce of the "starting point" of the final phase of SEELE's plans, according to the SDSS ADAM would had awoke one day, and with him the Angels. With 2I starting on their terms SEELE could take the initiative on when the Angel War would begin, and also could reduce ADAM into an embryonic state, making the more powerfull Angel out of the way and forcing all the others Angels to go for Lilith, which was protected by the Tokyo-3 fortress-city and the Evangelion they created meanwhile.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Nov 10, 2013 4:48 pm Honestly, I think "what did the Katsuragi Team think they were achieving?" is the more interesting question.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Nov 10, 2013 4:50 pm If the experiment really involved inserting human DNA into Adam (or Misato into Adam) then it is, essentially, what the angels were trying to do with Lilith, but the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 10, 2013 5:05 pm Rei IV wrote: Your fanfiction(s) answers that question rather well, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Nov 10, 2013 5:14 pm Reichu wrote: Wait... Were you one of the people I sent that too? I can never remember anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Nov 10, 2013 5:32 pm Reichu wrote: DON'T QUOTE THE ENTIRE POST YOU NUT

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 10, 2013 5:55 pm Rei IV wrote: This should answer your question and this as well .

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Nov 10, 2013 8:31 pm Reichu wrote: If Misato was the bloody DONOR for Adam's CE, you'd expect the narrative to actually address this in some way, as through flashbacks or establishing a concrete connection between Misato and Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 10, 2013 9:04 pm Atropos wrote: There's the scene with Misato observing Kaworu on the lake when it's shown he's somehow aware of her presence. Could suggest "clone telepathy" or something along those lines. There's also the way Kaworu is able to get close to Shinji in a way only Misato was capable of previously.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Mon Nov 11, 2013 4:54 am Ok, thanks for the replies. The "let's take the upperhand" approach makes sense. I like to think that at least a bit of a disaster was a good thing for Seele. Because, comes 2015, it could ensure that Nerv could say to the world "Look guys, we've seen these mofos before, it was in 2000, in fact we were waiting for more to come and that's why we built these super cool humanoid machines. Let us deal with them, we'll kick ass. If you don't let us take care of this, it'll be that Antarctica mess all over again and nobody wants that, right?". I think this could be a good reason (among the other ones), otherwise sure you'd want to have some fully developed Adam equivalents (i.e Evas) on your side before messing with the original one. I guess the SDSS provided some pretty accurate date for Adam's awakening and/or the Angels arrival, so there wasn't time to wait for the Evas to be ready and they tried the best they could with the Lance and the explosive device in Adam's S²... Clearly, things got out of hand because not having your amazing Lance o' Doom available for your Evas to use by the time the Angels come is not something you would want. I guess Kaworu can be considered and unexpected child of Adam? I guess it's plausible it had run out of souls/soul matter and that therefore Kaworu was created soulless, but then received Adam's own soul? This said, I always felt like Kaworu having 13-02-2000 as a birthdate was some sort of gag from Seele and/or something just meant to contribute to the spectators' suspicions about him...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 11, 2013 10:16 am I'm not entirely convinced that Adam wouldn't woken up anyway. I remember suggesting this offhandedly a while back, but it seems like it's been fanonized since then... Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: I guess Kaworu can be considered and unexpected child of Adam? I guess it's plausible it had run out of souls/soul matter and that therefore Kaworu was created soulless, but then received Adam's own soul?
